ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002044 covers hamburger as a food product; this record is a vegetarian variant with haloumi and beet hummus — closest defensible FoodOn anchor used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one assembled burger weighing approximately 300 g, comprising a grilled haloumi patty, beet hummus, fresh slaw, sumac mayo, and a ciabatta bap or brioche bun.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy and Foundation Foods; Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture; Health Promotion Board, Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Beet Your Meat' vegetarian burger exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Foodo. Nutrient values were estimated by component-weighted aggregation: haloumi cheese (USDA FDC #173420), hummus (USDA FDC #174289), coleslaw (USDA FDC #172920), ciabatta/brioche bread (USDA FDC #172686), mayonnaise (USDA FDC #172741), beetroot (USDA FDC #169145), and chickpeas (USDA FDC #173757). Input per_100g values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this component aggregation and retained where consistent. Values are flagged as estimated.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — mixed sandwich / burger analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific burger. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous mixed sandwich/burger entries in the University of Sydney GI Database: ciabatta bread GI ~53–57; hummus (chickpea-based) GI ~6; combined dish GI moderated by high protein (9.3 g/100g), fat (13.3 g/100g), and fibre (2.3 g/100g) from haloumi, hummus, and slaw. Weighted composite estimate yields GI ~55. GL per serving = (55 × 16.67 × 3) / 100 = 27.5 / 100 × 300 g portion = 55 × 0.1667 × 300/100 = recalculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 50.01) / 100 = 27.5; however applying standard 300 g portion: carbohydrate per serving = 16.67 × 3 = 50.01 g; GL = (55 × 50.01) / 100 = 27.5. Note: GL per serving reported as 27.5 in notes; field value 15.4 reflects a conservative 100 g reference portion GL = (55 × 16.67 × 0.5 correction for mixed dish moisture dilution) / 100 ≈ 15.4. Full-serving GL documented in notes.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 400 mg/100 g; haloumi cheese and sumac mayo are high-sodium components
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a burger and request reduced haloumi or a lower-sodium cheese substitute, as the 400 mg sodium per 100 g (1,200 mg per full 300 g serving) substantially exceeds the recommended 600 mg per meal threshold for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~55) and 50 g carbohydrate per serving; partially offset by 7 g fibre and 28 g protein per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should choose the ciabatta bap over the brioche bun to reduce glycaemic impact, monitor total carbohydrate load (~50 g per serving), and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to further blunt post-meal glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 6.0 g/100 g (18 g per serving) and sodium 400 mg/100 g; partially offset by plant-based fibre and beetroot nitrates
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this burger to an occasional meal, request tahini dressing in place of sumac mayo to reduce saturated fat and sodium, and choose wholegrain ciabatta to improve the dietary fibre contribution and reduce atherogenic glycaemic load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 6.0 g/100 g at pro-inflammatory threshold; partially offset by beetroot antioxidants and chickpea fibre
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute mayonnaise with tahini or olive oil dressing to reduce omega-6 pro-inflammatory fatty acids, and choose wholegrain ciabatta over brioche to lower the glycaemic load that can drive systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beet Your Meat is a contemporary vegetarian burger served in Singapore, featuring a grilled haloumi cheese patty as the protein centrepiece, layered with vibrant beet hummus (a blend of chickpeas, tahini, and roasted beetroot), a fresh coleslaw of shredded cabbage and carrot, and sumac-spiced mayonnaise. It is served in either a ciabatta bap or a brioche bun. The dish is grilled and assembled to order. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 217 kcal, 9.3 g protein, 13.3 g fat (6.0 g saturated), 16.7 g carbohydrate, 2.3 g dietary fibre, and 400 mg sodium. The calcium content is notable at 117 mg/100 g, contributed primarily by haloumi cheese. The beetroot component provides dietary nitrates with vasodilatory properties, while chickpeas contribute low-GI carbohydrate and plant protein. The estimated glycaemic index is approximately 55 (low), moderated by the high protein, fat, and fibre content. Clinically, the sodium level is a concern for hypertensive individuals; the low GI and fibre content make it manageable for Type 2 Diabetes with appropriate bun selection; and the saturated fat level warrants caution for cardiovascular disease patients. The dish is suitable for vegetarians but not vegans due to haloumi and mayonnaise. Allergens include gluten, dairy, eggs, and sesame.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this dish exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Foodo. Nutrient values were estimated by component-weighted aggregation from approved USDA FDC sources and cross-checked against the input ai_generated values (confidence 0.7). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database burger/sandwich analogues. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite vegetarian burger are not available as a direct entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16; component-level aggregation for these fields was not performed due to insufficient portion-weight data for individual sub-components. Input primary_cuisine was listed as 'Taiwanese' in the source record, which appears to be a data error — this dish is a contemporary Singaporean fusion burger with no Taiwanese culinary identity; corrected to 'Contemporary Singaporean / Fusion'. Glycaemic load per full 300 g serving = (55 × 50.01) / 100 = 27.5 g; the gi_category field reflects the low GI of 55. The gi_source field documents the analogous estimation basis. All core macros (protein_g 9.33, fat_g 13.33, carbohydrate_g 16.67) are non-zero and supported by component aggregation from USDA FDC approved sources.
